rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motiion And Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Leave To Intervene. The motion is continued to December 7, 2012, because the moving party failed to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers. The moving party shall provide those papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by noon on December 5. The Court has the opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 4, RESPONDENT DIGNITY HEALTH (FORMERLY NAMED CATHOLIC HEALTH DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Petitioner's allegations that Respondent Mercy is engaging in an illegal kickback scheme support the claims. Exhibit A does not contradict petitioner's allegations. Under Mike Moore's 24-Hour Towing v. City of San Diego (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1294, 1305, the Court does not defer to a legislative determination where it is arbitrary, fraudulent, and/or illegal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTS GOLDEN EMPIRE MANAGED CARE, A MEDICAL GROUP, MANAGED CARE SYSTEMS, LP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff's allegations that defendants jointly entered into an illegal kickback scheme support the claims. The collateral estoppel argument fails because there is no showing that the federal court ever addressed the state law issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MECHANICS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN T. KONTRABECKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtors
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MECHANICS BANK'S Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtors. Denied, without prejudice. "The notice of the motion shall be served on the judgment debtor. Service shall be made personally or by mail." (CCP § 708.510(b).) Plaintiff's service of the notice of motion by overnight delivery is procedurally defective. (See Notice of Motion, Proof of Service, filed Oct. 25, 2012.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Req. For An Order To Compel Pltf'S Independent Mental Examination And For An Order To Compel Production Fo Moca Raw Data
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S Joinder In Request For An Order To Compel Plaintiff'S Independent Mental Examination And For An Order To Compel Production For MOCA Raw Data. The motion is denied without prejudice. The Court denies the request for an independent mental examination because defendants do not provide sufficient information regarding the proposed examination. Given the lack of information in this motion, the Court cannot issue an order in compliance with CCP 2032.320(d). The Court denies the request for the MOCA data. Defendants should comply with the production request procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 16, Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions. Continued on the Court's own motion to February 5, 2013 in order for the parties to comply with the ruling issued on September 24, 2012. The parties' statements filed on October 22, 23 and 24, 2012 respectively fail to comply with this ruling. Therefore, the parties shall further meet and confer in person to address each issue in dispute with respect to each discovery request and response. For each motion, the parties are ordered to jointly prepare and submit by January 25, 2013 a joint separate statement of issues remaining in dispute. As to each unresolved discovery request, the statement(s) shall set forth: (1) the request of Plaintiff (updated to reflect any modifications made by plaintiff through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (2) the response of Defendant to the request (updated to reflect any modifications made by the defendant through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (3) Plaintiff's position as to the adequacy of the Defendant's response; and (4) Defendant's position as to each request and its response. The statement(s) shall be signed by both parties. In addition, the parties shall submit proposed orders with respect to the discovery issues remaining in dispute, including as necessary protocols for addressing privilege, privacy, and electronically stored information. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ASSET ACCEPTANCE, LLC VS. BETTY DELEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Exclude Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 5, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CARMY DELEON Notice Of Motion To Exclude Evidence, Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt
